channel.title: Which one should we use? react-imported-component vs loadable-components
channel.description:
@orballo could you please do this **Which one should we use?** between `react-imported-component` and `loadable-components` for our code splitting?
Something like this: https://community.frontity.org/t/which-one-should-we-use-emotion-vs-styled-components/27/5
I've just discovered loadable-components and it looks great!
I've already done the size: - `npx package-size @loadable/component --analyze`: 15kb - `npx package-size react-imported-component --analyze`: 19kb

item.title: Which one should we use? react-imported-component vs loadable-components
item.dc:creator: luisherranz
item.description:
Well, actually… there’s no need to close. Maybe someone wants to say something in the future.

Read full topic
item.source: Which one should we use? react-imported-component vs loadable-components

item.title: Which one should we use? react-imported-component vs loadable-components
item.dc:creator: luisherranz
item.description:
Closing again

Read full topic
item.source: Which one should we use? react-imported-component vs loadable-components

item.title: Which one should we use? react-imported-component vs loadable-components
item.dc:creator: luisherranz
item.description:
I was wrong, loadable-components works with HMR. There’s a confusion in that issue about naming: it does work with HMR but it does not work with RHL (react-hot-loader).

We’ve decided not to use RHL so everything is fine

Read full topic
item.source: Which one should we use? react-imported-component vs loadable-components

item.title: Which one should we use? react-imported-component vs loadable-components
item.dc:creator: luisherranz
item.description:
loadable-components doesn’t work with HMR so I’m looking at react-imported-component again.

Read full topic

item.title: Which one should we use? react-imported-component vs loadable-components
item.dc:creator: luisherranz
item.description:
Ok, loadable-components is the winner then

Edit: I’m studying it again.

Read full topic
item.source: Which one should we use? react-imported-component vs loadable-components

item.title: Which one should we use? react-imported-component vs loadable-components
item.dc:creator: orballo
item.description:
Yes, I think so

Read full topic
item.source: Which one should we use? react-imported-component vs loadable-components

item.title: Which one should we use? react-imported-component vs loadable-components
item.dc:creator: luisherranz
item.description:
I think we can already declare a winner here, can we @orballo?

Read full topic
item.source: Which one should we use? react-imported-component vs loadable-components

item.title: Which one should we use? react-imported-component vs loadable-components
item.dc:creator: orballo
item.description:
luisherranz:

BTW, @orballo I tried bundlephobia but I didn’t like it. Sometimes I got different results than using npx package-size xxx --analyze which I think it’s the most accurate one. Even npx package-size xxx (without --analyze ) gave me weird results sometimes.

Ok, I tried it without the --analyze options and returned the same results as bundlephobia. I used bundlephobia because it has info about the bundle.

luisherranz:

Try loadable-components only. I really dislike having to maintain a app/imported.js file. It complicates our extensibility.

Oki doki.

Read full topic
item.source: Which one should we use? react-imported-component vs loadable-components

item.title: Which one should we use? react-imported-component vs loadable-components
item.dc:creator: luisherranz
item.description:
orballo:

I will try both of them to better decide on what to use.

Try loadable-components only. I really dislike having to maintain a app/imported.js file. It complicates our extensibility.

Read full topic
item.source: Which one should we use? react-imported-component vs loadable-components

item.title: Which one should we use? react-imported-component vs loadable-components
item.dc:creator: luisherranz
item.description:
orballo:

To make SSR work, we need to run a “build” command to generate a file with all the import() s of the project. This would look something like "generate-imported-component": "imported-components app app/imported.ts" in our package.json . I really don’t fancy this.

Uh, I don’t really like it either. We can’t do that.

BTW, @orballo I tried bundlephobia but I didn’t like it. Sometimes I got different results than using npx package-size xxx --analyze which I think is the most accurate one. Even npx package-size xxx (without --analyze) gave me weird results sometimes.

Read full topic
item.source: Which one should we use? react-imported-component vs loadable-components

item.title: Which one should we use? react-imported-component vs loadable-components
item.dc:creator: orballo
item.description:
Ok, here we go!

react-imported-component

GitHub

theKashey/react-imported-component

✂️📦Bundler-independent solution for SSR-friendly code-splitting - theKashey/react-imported-component

  • Documentation
    I find the documentation a bit chaotic, also it looks like the creator doesn’t speak English fluently and sometimes can be difficult to understand.
    There is one example for SSR but it doesn’t work out of the box however I think we can make it work.

  • Features
    I think it works a bit different compare to react-universal-component, which we are used to.
    To make SSR work, we need to run a “build” command to generate a file with all the import()s of the project. This would look something like "generate-imported-component": "imported-components app app/imported.ts" in our package.json. I really don’t fancy this.
    I find the API naming a bit confusing.

  • React Suspense
    Supported

  • Typescript
    Supported

  • Community
    GitHub Stars: 200
    NPM Downloads: 24,768
    Contributors: 6

  • Bundle size
    9 kB (https://bundlephobia.com/result?p=react-imported-component@5.5.1)

loadable-components

GitHub

gregberge/loadable-components

The recommended Code Splitting library for React ✂️✨ - gregberge/loadable-components

  • Documentation
    Looks much better than the other one, and I think it covers all our needs. We can find an SSR example but I couldn’t try it yet.

  • Features
    It works similar to react-universal-component so it will be easier to adopt.
    It is the recommended library in React docs.

  • React Suspense
    Supported

  • Typescript
    Supported (https://github.com/smooth-code/loadable-components/issues/79)

  • Community
    GitHub Starts: 1,685
    NPM Downloads: 24,286
    Contributors: 41

  • Bundle size
    7.3 kB (https://bundlephobia.com/result?p=@loadable/component@5.6.0) +
    76.4 kB (https://bundlephobia.com/result?p=@loadable/server@5.6.0) (92% of this package is lodash, maybe this can be fixed with tree shaking) = 83.7 kB

I will try both of them to better decide on what to use.

Click to view the poll.

Read full topic
item.source: Which one should we use? react-imported-component vs loadable-components

item.title: Which one should we use? react-imported-component vs loadable-components
item.dc:creator: luisherranz
item.description:
@orballo could you please do this Which one should we use? between react-imported-component and loadable-components for our code splitting?

Something like this: Which one should we use? emotion vs styled-components

I’ve just discovered loadable-components and it looks great!

I’ve already done the size:

  • npx package-size @loadable/component --analyze: 15kb
  • npx package-size react-imported-component --analyze: 19kb

Read full topic
item.source: Which one should we use? react-imported-component vs loadable-components
